articles.title_en: Georgy Pirogov fled Russia after the invasion of Ukraine. He later vanished on a job in Uzbekistan and resurfaced in a Moscow jail. Now he’s been sentenced to 23 years.
articles.lead_en: The Moscow City Court has sentenced Georgy Pirogov, a 37-year-old man from the Moscow region, to 23 years in a maximum-security penal colony, the independent Russian news outlet Mediazona reported, citing a source familiar with the ruling. The court also fined him 800,000 rubles.

articles.title_en: Miroshnik: resignation of the Cabinet will allow Zelensky to abdicate responsibility
articles.lead_en: The Ambassador at Large of the Russian Foreign Ministry called the resignation part of Vladimir Zelensky's anti-crisis to deflect accusations of corruption.

articles.title_en: Miroshnik: the candidate for prime minister of Ukraine knows how to “effectively master” Western financial assistance

articles.title_en: Miroshnik: the decision to change the Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine was made under pressure from external forces
articles.lead_en: Prime Minister Yulia Sviridenko is known as a close associate of the former head of the office of Vladimir Zelensky Andrei Ermak, who was involved in a corruption scandal, noted the Ambassador at Large of the Russian Foreign Ministry
